title: Model Performance Degradation Detection (Art. 12)

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run, then normalised in Phase A (clause 'EU AI Act' → 'Art.15'). Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).

app: Splunk Machine Learning Toolkit (2890), Splunk ITSI (1841)
spl:
index=ai_metrics sourcetype="ai:model:kpi" metric_name="accuracy" earliest=-7d
| timechart span=1h perc95(metric_value) as p95_acc by model_id
| untable _time model_id p95_acc
| eventstats median(p95_acc) as baseline by model_id
| eval delta=p95_acc-baseline
| where delta < -0.02
| table _time, model_id, p95_acc, baseline, delta
value: Detects sudden drops in accuracy/auroc vs baseline to support operational logging and risk management for high-risk AI performance stability.
implementation: (1) Ingest batch evaluation KPIs hourly; (2) tune -0.02 threshold per model risk file; (3) create ITSI KPI thresholding; (4) document rollback criteria in QMS.
visualization: Time chart (p95_acc vs baseline), Table (negative deltas), Single value (models in alert)
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
